messages.content: when was the british invasion

The British Invasion typically refers to a period in the mid-1960s when British rock and pop bands, such as the Beatles, the Rolling Stones, and the Who, achieved massive commercial success in the United States. The exact date of the British Invasion is a matter of debate, but it is generally considered to have begun in early 1964 and continued through the mid-1960s.

During the 1950s, most African American musicians were less successful in mainstream markets than white artists covering their records.

Capitol records was hesitant to take on the Beatles' music in the US due to previous bad track records by UK artists in America.
Group of answer choices

True

False
False. Capitol Records was initially hesitant to take on the Beatles' music in the US, but it was due to lack of interest from other record labels in the UK rather than previous bad track records.

what genre was the bryds
The Byrds were primarily a folk rock band, but they also incorporated elements of psychedelic rock and country rock into their music.

where did top 40 radio start

Top 40 radio is believed to have originated in the United States, specifically in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, in the late 1940s. Radio station WABC in New York City is also credited with popularizing the format in the 1950s.
who founded motown
Motown was founded by Berry Gordy Jr. in Detroit, Michigan in 1959.

who made baby love
"Baby Love" is a song by the American girl group The Supremes. It was released in 1964 and produced by Motown's main production team, Holland–Dozier–Holland.
california girls by the beach boys is a type of british invasion?

No, "California Girls" by The Beach Boys is not a type of British Invasion. British Invasion typically refers to the period in the mid-1960s when British rock and pop bands, such as the Beatles, the Rolling Stones, and the Who, achieved massive commercial success in the United States. "California Girls" by The Beach Boys is a song that celebrates the beauty and allure of California women, and it was released in 1965, around the same time as the British Invasion. However, it is not a type of British Invasion music.
is last train to clarksville have the intent to sound and look like a british invasion band

The song "Last Train to Clarksville" by The Monkees was released in 1966, during the height of the British Invasion. The Monkees were an American band, and while the song may have had some influences from British pop and rock music, it was not specifically intended to sound or look like a British Invasion band. 

The Monkees were actually formed as part of a television show aimed at creating an American version of The Beatles and cashing in on the popularity of British Invasion bands at the time. However, The Monkees eventually developed their own sound and style, which was a mix of pop, rock, and folk music. "Last Train to Clarksville" is an example of their early work and helped to establish the band's popularity. So, while it may have been influenced by British Invasion bands, it was not intended to sound or look like one.
